channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: States move to ban or restrict use of synthetic dyes used in candies and cereal
item.description: Synthetic dyes used to make brightly colored cereals, drinks and candies are coming under scrutiny in states across the country.
item.dc:creator: Leah Willingham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: TECOLUCA, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are gang members have been held since their removal from the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ALEX BRANDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE slashes billions more in expenses for programs like Peruvian climate change and gender equity in Mexico
item.description: DOGE announced it slashed 113 contracts valued at $4.7 billion for $3.3 billion in savings for programs like climate change in Peru and gender equity in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge accused the Justice Department on Wednesday of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration's criticism of the courts for rulings that blocked parts of the president'
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is partially backtracking on a plan that would require all new and existing beneficiaries to travel to a Social Security field office to verify their identity.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blue state sanctuary laws enabled illegal 'abolish ICE' activist to evade capture, says local DA
item.description: Colorado District Attorney George Brauchler is speaking out against sanctuary policies in his state that he says enabled activist Jeanette Vizguerra to evade capture, “making a mockery of the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration asks SCOTUS to approve DEI-related education cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to approve it slashing hundreds of millions of dollars in grants from the Education Department as part of its DEI crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge struggles with scope of relief for fired workers
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge in Maryland said Wednesday he will at least briefly extend a temporary order requiring the Trump administration to bring back federal workers who were fired as part of a dramatic downsizing of the federal workforce, but the judge said he was struggling with the scope
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep Jasmine Crockett refuses to apologize for 'Gov Hot Wheels' comment
item.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett stormed away from an interview with FOX Business after being asked about calling Gov. Greg Abbott "Governor Hot Wheels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries calls for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries tells NewsNation that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should lose his job.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republicans look to DOGE model for state government efficiency commission
item.description: The commission would consist of a panel of six citizens who would investigate fraud in state agencies and potential conflicts of interest in the Legislature. Three members would be appointed by Republicans and three by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: NYC Council to vote on new rules for scaffolding, sidewalk sheds
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council is set to take up a series of bills aimed at cracking down on scaffolding and sidewalk sheds.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump officials texted war plans to a group chat in a secure app that included a journalist
item.description: Top national security officials for President Donald Trump, including his defense secretary, texted war plans for upcoming military strikes in Yemen to a group chat in a secure messaging app that included the editor-in-chief for The Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Lawmakers advance legislation to lower the gun-buying age
item.description: For years, lawmakers have tried to overturn a Florida law banning individuals under 21 from buying guns.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House: 'Critical functions' will stay under Department of Education
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said all the critical functions of the Department of Education will remain ahead of President Trump signing an executive order to dismantle the federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Florida bill could raise highway speed limits
item.description: Highway speeds in Florida could be raised if a new bill passes through the Florida Legislature and makes it to the governor's desk.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans want Musk to shut up about Social Security
item.description: Senate Republicans want Elon Musk to stop talking about Social Security, and the Department of Government Efficiency to leave it alone.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ creating path for people with criminal convictions to own guns again
item.description: The Justice Department plans to create a process for those with criminal convictions to restore their gun rights, sparking alarm it will return firearms to those convicted of violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
